ddls:
CREATE TABLE "streams" (
  "stream_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each stream */,
  "hw_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the hardware associated with the stream */,
  "vm_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the virtual machine where the stream is running */,
  "process_id" INTEGER /* Process identifier for the monitored process */,
  "context_id" INTEGER /* Context identifier for the process execution context */,
  "priority" INTEGER /* Priority level of the process (high, normal, low) */,
  "flag" INTEGER /* Flag indicating the status or state of the process */,
  "start_time" TEXT /* Timestamp when the stream started */,
  "end_time" TEXT /* Timestamp when the stream ended (if applicable) */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the stream */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Any additional notes or comments about the stream */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("stream_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_streams_priority FOREIGN KEY ("priority") REFERENCES priorities ("priority_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_streams_flag FOREIGN KEY ("flag") REFERENCES flags ("flag_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_streams_vm_id FOREIGN KEY ("vm_id") REFERENCES virtual_machines ("vm_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_streams_context_id FOREIGN KEY ("context_id") REFERENCES contexts ("context_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_streams_process_id FOREIGN KEY ("process_id") REFERENCES processes ("process_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_streams_hw_id FOREIGN KEY ("hw_id") REFERENCES hardware ("hw_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "hardware" (
  "hw_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each hardware component */,
  "hw_name" TEXT /* Name of the hardware component */,
  "hw_type" TEXT /* Type of hardware (e.g., Server, Router) */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the hardware (active, inactive, maintenance) */,
  "manufacturer" TEXT /* Name of the hardware manufacturer */,
  "model" TEXT /* Model number of the hardware */,
  "serial_number" TEXT /* Serial number of the hardware */,
  "location" TEXT /* Physical location of the hardware */,
  "last_maintenance" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last maintenance performed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("hw_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "virtual_machines" (
  "vm_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each virtual machine */,
  "vm_name" TEXT /* Name of the virtual machine */,
  "vm_type" TEXT /* Type of virtual machine (e.g., Development, Production) */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the virtual machine (running, stopped) */,
  "os_type" TEXT /* Type of operating system (e.g., Windows, Linux) */,
  "os_version" TEXT /* Version of the operating system */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the virtual machine */,
  "last_backup" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last backup */,
  "cpu_cores" INTEGER /* Number of CPU cores */,
  "memory_gb" INTEGER /* Amount of memory in GB */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("vm_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "processes" (
  "process_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each process */,
  "process_name" TEXT /* Name of the process */,
  "start_time" TEXT /* Time when the process started */,
  "end_time" TEXT /* Time when the process ended (if applicable) */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the process (running, completed, failed) */,
  "last_heartbeat" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last heartbeat signal from the process */,
  "error_count" INTEGER /* Number of errors encountered by the process */,
  "resource_utilization" REAL /* Average resource utilization (CPU, memory) */,
  "dependencies" TEXT /* List of dependent processes (JSON array) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("process_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "contexts" (
  "context_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each context */,
  "context_description" TEXT /* Description of the execution context */,
  "creation_time" TEXT /* Time when the context was created */,
  "last_modified" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last modification to the context */,
  "owner" INTEGER /* ID of the user who owns the context */,
  "environment" TEXT /* Environment in which the context is running (e.g., Production, Development) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("context_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_contexts_owner FOREIGN KEY ("owner") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "priorities" (
  "priority_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each priority level */,
  "priority_name" TEXT /* Name of the priority level (e.g., High, Normal, Low) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the priority level */,
  "default_flag" INTEGER /* Default flag ID for this priority level */,
  "max_cpu_usage" REAL /* Maximum allowed CPU usage for processes with this priority */,
  "max_memory_usage" REAL /* Maximum allowed memory usage for processes with this priority */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("priority_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "flags" (
  "flag_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each flag */,
  "flag_name" TEXT /* Name of the flag */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the flag indicating process state */,
  "severity" TEXT /* Severity level of the flag (e.g., Low, Medium, High) */,
  "action_required" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if action is required when this flag is set */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("flag_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, IT staff, monitor) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last login */,
  "active" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the user account is active */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department to which the user belongs */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alerts" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert */,
  "stream_id" INTEGER /* ID of the stream that triggered the alert */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who received the alert */,
  "alert_time" TEXT /* Time when the alert was generated */,
  "alert_message" TEXT /* Message detailing the alert */,
  "severity" TEXT /* Severity level of the alert (e.g., Low, Medium, High) */,
  "read_status" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the alert has been read */,
  "action_taken" TEXT /* Description of the action taken in response to the alert */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_stream_id FOREIGN KEY ("stream_id") REFERENCES streams ("stream_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "performance_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each performance log */,
  "stream_id" INTEGER /* ID of the stream associated with the log */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the recorded performance data */,
  "cpu_usage" REAL /* CPU usage percentage at the time of logging */,
  "memory_usage" REAL /* Memory usage in MB at the time of logging */,
  "disk_usage" REAL /* Disk usage in GB at the time of logging */,
  "network_usage" REAL /* Network usage in MB/s at the time of logging */,
  "io_operations" INTEGER /* Number of I/O operations at the time of logging */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_logs_stream_id FOREIGN KEY ("stream_id") REFERENCES streams ("stream_id")
)
